sub_items.label: “Where AI gets real,” a partner who turns AI models and off-the-shelf agents into solutions that create tangible results
“AI that works for you,” a partner who bridges the raw power of AI with the realities of your business to engineer solutions that create measurable impact
“Context is everything,” a partner who takes your company’s DNA—its data, workflows and institutional knowledge—and builds systems tailored to your unique context
“Beyond intelligence,” a partner who integrates, customizes and builds what’s needed to unlock the full power and potential of AI
“Built for this,” a partner with a long history of shifting ahead of shifting client needs, helping businesses adapt and stay ahead, in the AI era and always
“Force multiplier,” a partner who helps you augment, empower and multiply your talent with the digital labor of AI and agents
“AI builders,” a partner who co-creates purpose-built AI platforms, applications and tools customized for your business’ unique needs and ambitions

[Q43]: How would you characterize the relationship between your organization's AI ambitions and its AI capabilities?

answer_codes.2: Small gap – Capabilities are close to what’s needed to achieve our goals
answer_codes.3: Moderate gap - Some additional capabilities needed
answer_codes.4: Large gap - Significant additional capabilities needed
answer_codes.5: Very large gap - Ambitions far exceed capabilities

question_text: [Q44]: Which statement best describes your company’s outlook on the impact AI is likely to have on your industry?

answer_codes.1: Existential threat – Organizations must adapt or fail
answer_codes.2: Major disruption – Organizations require fundamental changes to succeed
answer_codes.3: Moderate disruption – Organizations require significant but manageable changes
answer_codes.4: Incremental change – Organizations require improvements but not wholesale transformation
answer_codes.6: Not able to predict level of disruption
